delay_description: Other prerequisite — planning, renovation, refurbishment, or other blocking event

trigger_phrases.phrase: refurbishment starting that week, six weeks
house refurb with a roof delay

typical_timeline.notes: Based on 4 timeline signals observed: 6 weeks from ~mid-May, a few weeks, 1 month LTL trial planned, September 2026

how_to_handle_in_meeting.acknowledge_phrase: Understood — you need [the works] to be done first before you can think about guests.
how_to_handle_in_meeting.timeline_question: What's the current timeline on [the renovation / planning / other]? And is that in your control or dependent on someone else?
how_to_handle_in_meeting.reframe_approach: Separate the decisions: 'You don't need to resolve the [renovation] before signing the agreement — you can sign now and go live when the property is ready. The agreement doesn't start until you go live.'
how_to_handle_in_meeting.how_to_keep_engaged: Get a realistic completion date for the blocker. Set a calendar reminder for 4 weeks before that date. 'I'll check in with you in [N] weeks to see how the [works] are coming along.'
how_to_handle_in_meeting.what_to_write_to_monday: Describe the specific blocker in Call Brief. Set date_mm2vmj1q to midpoint of expected resolution window.

conversion_after_delay.drop_off_reason: Insufficient data to determine — fewer than 10 delay entries per type
conversion_after_delay.note: First run — conversion-after-delay rates will improve with more data over time

do_not_attempt_offer_because: Renovation, planning, or mortgage decisions are external to the lead's control. Offering while they're waiting on something else creates the impression you're not listening.
